pages.raw_text:
When is boss not boss, was the
conundrum propounded by Barber Ben
son when he appeared before the
Trades council last night and wanted
to know why he could not be allowed
to work his own shop after the closing
hours set by the union. 1t seems that
q boss after he is affiliated with unions
is no boss at all. Benson may work
"a hours out of each day if he go
desires but he cannot do so and still
conduct.a union shop. Benson argued
and argued and so did the representa-
tives of the BARRIERS union. It finished
with tho trades council endorsing the
action of the BARRIERS when they de-
clared tho Benson shop unfair to organ
lied labor.


The trouble is of long standing. Ben
son continues to work after o'clock
in the evening while the union rules
only allow union shops open until Z.
This has caused the trouble. Then the
union at it's meeting on February 8,
declared the Benson shop unfair, and
now the trades council endorses its
stand.
